items.summary: Command injection in GlobalProtect on PAN-OS permitted unauthenticated remote code execution as root. Exploited in the wild. Fixed in 10.2.9-h1, 11.0.4-h1, 11.1.2-h3 and later; hot-patches issued for older trains.

Authentication bypass in PAN-OS web management interface allowed unauthenticated network-attacker admin access. Mitigation: restrict mgmt-plane access to trusted hosts; upgrade to 10.2.12-h2 / 11.0.6-h1 / 11.1.5-h1 / 11.2.4-h1.

"Citrix Bleed" — sensitive memory disclosure from NetScaler Gateway / AAA VIPs, including session cookies enabling session hijack. Active ransomware exploitation Q4 2023. Fixed in 13.1-49.15, 13.0-92.19, and later; revoke all active sessions after patching.
Authenticated remote code execution on NetScaler management interface when configured as Gateway/AAA vserver. Requires mgmt-plane access. Fixed in 14.1-12.35, 13.1-51.15, 13.0-92.21.

Unauthenticated brute-force of remote-access VPN credentials via a flaw in how ASA/FTD handled auth requests. Abused by Akira ransomware. Mitigation: MFA, default group lockout, disable default WebVPN connection profile; fixes in later 9.16/9.17/9.18/9.19 trains.

Web UI privilege-escalation flaw letting an unauthenticated attacker create a privilege-15 local account. Exploited in the wild together with CVE-2023-20273. Fix: upgrade IOS XE and disable the HTTP server (no ip http server / secure-server) if not required.
Command injection in IOS XE web UI that lets an authenticated user (e.g. the account created via CVE-2023-20198) write files and gain root. Chained with 20198 for full device takeover.
DoS via crafted HTTP request to the WebVPN service on ASA/FTD, causing device reload. Reported alongside ArcaneDoor campaign. Fixed in 9.16.4.60, 9.18.4.24, 9.20.2.6 and later.
Authenticated command-injection in Cisco Integrated Management Controller web interface. Fix: upgrade IMC per hardware model's matrix; restrict IMC network reachability to management VLAN.

Authentication bypass allowing AD domain members belonging to an attacker-created 'ESX Admins' group to gain full ESXi admin access. Exploited by ransomware. Mitigation: remove AD integration if not used, or explicitly set the admin group; fix in ESXi 8.0 U3.
Heap-overflow in DCERPC protocol implementation enabling remote code execution via crafted network packets. No auth required. Fixed in vCenter 8.0 U3b / 8.0 U2d / 7.0 U3t.
Use-after-free in nf_tables leading to local privilege escalation to root. Public exploit since early 2024. Patched across all stable trees; audit any host allowing unprivileged user namespaces.

"regreSSHion" — signal-handler race in sshd on glibc systems allowing unauthenticated RCE on 32-bit builds; difficult but feasible on 64-bit. Fixed in OpenSSH 9.8p1; mitigate with LoginGraceTime 0 until patched.
Denial of service via X.509 certificate policy constraints — malicious chain can cause exponential CPU use during verification. Fixed in 1.1.1t, 3.0.8, 3.1.0.
NTLM hash disclosure via crafted file — triggered by minimal user interaction (selecting/right-clicking a file). Exploited in the wild. Patched in the November 2024 cumulative update.
"LDAPNightmare" — unauthenticated DoS / RCE pre-conditions via LDAP client path; crash domain controllers via crafted DSR referral. Patch from December 2024 cumulative update.
Zero-click remote code execution via crafted IPv6 packets processed by the Windows TCP/IP stack. Mitigate by disabling IPv6 on exposed hosts until patched; fix in August 2024 cumulative.
GVCP dissector infinite loop crashing Wireshark during capture analysis. Fix: upgrade to 4.0.12 / 4.2.2 or later. Impact limited to analyst workstations.
